title: Mohlana secunda
intro: Mohlana secunda is a plant species commonly known as 'Mountain tea' or 'Mohlana', belonging to the family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including its ability to treat fever, cough, and colds due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Mohlana secunda include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, and is also used in some traditional remedies for respiratory issues and as a digestive aid.

uses: Mohlana secunda has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. It is also known to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in soothing and healing wounds. The plant's extracts have been used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Additionally, Mohlana secunda is believed to have antiseptic properties, making it effective in preventing infections. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of its medicinal uses.
